question.text: Nisha submits a redemption request for her equity fund at 3:15 PM on Thursday. What NAV applies?

answer.text: Thursday's closing NAV (same day, since it's within business hours)
Wednesday's closing NAV (the previous business day)
Monday's closing NAV (the next week's first business day)
Friday's closing NAV (the next business day)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since Nisha submitted after the 3 PM cut-off on Thursday, the applicable NAV is the closing NAV of the next business day, which is Friday. Had she submitted before 3 PM, she would have gotten Thursday's closing NAV. **The Trap:** 3:15 PM is after the 3 PM cut-off. Even 1 minute past cut-off pushes the applicable NAV to the next business day. 'Within business hours' is irrelevant -- only the 3 PM cut-off matters. **Cross-Exam Context:** This scenario tests the hard cut-off rule. RTAs use time stamps to verify whether applications were received before or after 3 PM. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A 15-minute delay cost Nisha a full day's NAV difference. In falling markets, this delay might actually benefit her (lower redemption NAV gives more units' worth), but in rising markets, it's a loss. Always aim for well-before-3 PM submission.
